content: Just as the linguistic meaning of 'reflexion' is rooted in its double definition as both physical light reflection and deliberate cognitive introspection in human learning, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be improved by explicitly designing a dual functional 'reflexion' layer that first mirrors (reflects) the raw inconsistency of the LLM’s existing divergent solving perspectives, then performs intentional reflective reasoning to refine outputs based on that mirrored feedback. This dual-layer design draws directly on the dual semantic root of the term 'reflexion' itself that has been taught in human language learning for decades.
